summary: Drew DeVault provides his June 2020 status update covering three main projects. aerc, the terminal email client, shipped version 0.4.0 with contributions including a scrollable folder list and PGP improvements. wlroots continues its work as a unified multiplexer for Linux display and input subsystems via the Wayland protocol. SourceHut is gaining GraphQL API support and undergoing Alpine 3.12 infrastructure upgrades.
key_points: aerc 0.4.0 released with scrollable folder list and PGP bugfixes, driven by community contributions
wlroots serves as a critical integration layer unifying DRM/KMS, GBM, OpenGL, libinput, and udev under the Wayland protocol
SourceHut is adopting GraphQL APIs with a JavaScript-free playground, starting with meta.sr.ht
Upcoming SourceHut work includes overhauled authentication, OAuth2, and improved webhooks
money_quotes: wlroots is a crucial core component tying together a lot of different parts of the ecosystem — DRM/KMS, GBM, OpenGL, libinput, udev, and more — bringing together integrations for many disparate systems and providing a single unified multiplexer for them over the Wayland protocol.
Taking full advantage of all of these systems and becoming a more perfect integration of them is a long-term goal.
We are working hard to squeeze every drop of performance out of your system.
The main development improvements in this respect which have occured since the last status updates are the introduction of a JavaScript-free GraphQL playground.
Coming improvements will include an overhaul to authentication and OAuth2 support, and a dramatically improved approach to webhooks.
key_insight: wlroots is maturing as a unified integration layer for the Linux graphics and input stack, while SourceHut's GraphQL migration signals a significant architectural evolution for the platform.
